# commit split --no-restack leaves upstack branches untouched.

[!unix] skip # pending github.com/creack/pty/pull/155
[!git:2.52] skip # git add -p output is fixed to Git version

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-07-08T05:04:32Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

git add feature1.txt feature2.txt
gs branch create features -m 'Add features'
git add feature3.txt
gs branch create feature3 -m 'Add feature 3'

git rev-parse --short feature3
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature3-before.txt

gs down

# Split the lower branch through the real interactive staging workflow.
with-term -final exit $WORK/input.txt -- gs commit split --no-restack -m 'Add feature 1'

# Splitting rewrites features; feature3 keeps its original head when the
# upstack restack is disabled.
git rev-parse --short feature3
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature3-before.txt

-- repo/feature1.txt --
feature 1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
feature 2
-- repo/feature3.txt --
feature 3
-- input.txt --
await feature1
feed y\r
await feature2
feed q\r
-- golden/feature3-before.txt --
16e1af8
